# Support

Thanks for using OMNIX. This page is the single place that routes you to the
right channel. Please use the channel that matches your need.

## Usage, evaluation, and "how do I…" questions

Open a [GitHub issue](https://github.com/gowdaharshith1998-lang/OMNIX/issues)
using the **Bug report** or **Feature request** template under
[`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`](ISSUE_TEMPLATE). Search existing issues first — your
question may already be answered.

Before opening an issue, the documentation often has the answer:

- [README](../README.md) — overview and quickstart.
- [ARCHITECTURE](../ARCHITECTURE.md) — how the system fits together.
- [docs/](../docs/README.md) — full documentation index, including the
  [data-migration runbook](../docs/dm/runbook.md) and
  [M1 demo walkthrough](../docs/M1_DEMO.md).

## Bug reports and feature requests

Use the issue templates:

- **Bug report** — unexpected behavior, with steps to reproduce.
- **Feature request** — a capability you'd like to see.
- **Docs cleanup** — documentation that is wrong, stale, or unclear.

## Commercial licensing and private pilots

OMNIX is source-available, not OSI open source. For commercial use, hosted/managed
use, private-pilot scoping, or evaluation under a commercial license, open an issue
labeled for licensing (see the contact links in
[`.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml`](ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml)) or email
**gowdaharshith1998@gmail.com**. See [LICENSE](../LICENSE) for what the
source-available license does and does not permit.

## Security vulnerabilities

**Do not** open a public issue for a security problem. Follow
[SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md) — report privately via a GitHub security advisory
or email. See that policy for the supported versions and disclosure process.

## Conduct concerns

See the [Code of Conduct](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). Reports are handled privately.
